summary: The IDF reported killing three senior Palestinian Islamic Jihad militants and striking a petrochemical complex in Mahshahr, Iran. Hezbollah launched three rockets toward Israeli forces in southern Lebanon, while Iran announced a cessation of operations against Israel but threatened harsher measures if attacks continue. Multiple alerts and sirens were issued in Eilat and northern Israel following reports of UAV incursions and potential ballistic missile launches from Iran.
key_points: IDF killed three senior Palestinian Islamic Jihad militants over the weekend (Source: @idf_telegram/24238).
Israel’s air force struck multiple Iranian air‑defence sites and a petrochemical complex in Mahshahr, southwest Iran (Source: @idf_telegram/24232).
Hezbollah fired three rockets toward Israeli troops in southern Lebanon, two intercepted and one landing near forces (Source: @manniefabian/58511).
Iran announced a formal cessation of armed operations against Israel but warned of far harsher measures if attacks resume (Source: @shin_persian/6748).
Ballistic missiles were detected launching from Iran toward Israel, prompting sirens in northern Israel (Source: @manniefabian/58500).
Eilat authorities issued alerts after a possible UAV intrusion and a suspected missile launch from Yemen (Source: @N12chat/211771).
Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu warned that any Iranian‑linked attack from Lebanon will be met with strong retaliation (Source: @amitsegal/61975).

sections.executive_summary: The IDF killed three senior Palestinian Islamic Jihad militants and struck a petrochemical complex in Mahshahr, Iran, marking a significant escalation. Hezbollah launched three rockets toward Israeli forces in southern Lebanon, with two intercepted and one landing near troops. Iran announced a cessation of operations against Israel while simultaneously threatening harsher retaliation if attacks continue.

points: The Israeli Air Force conducted precise strikes on nine Iranian air‑defence systems in western and central Iran, including a site armed with anti‑aircraft missiles, as part of operation "Shagat Haari" (Source: @idf_telegram/24232).

Dozens of Israeli fighter jets also hit three factories at a petrochemical complex in southwest Iran, targeting facilities linked to the Iranian regime’s missile program (Source: @idf_telegram/24232).

Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps announced the cessation of armed operations against Israel, but warned that any continued aggression, especially in southern Lebanon, would trigger significantly stronger measures (Source: @shin_persian/6748).
Iranian officials warned of "severe and crushing steps" in response to Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon, indicating a readiness to expand retaliation (Source: @almogboker78/17418).
Ballistic missiles were detected launching from Iran toward Israel, prompting alerts in northern Israel; the IDF reported interception of most missiles (Source: @manniefabian/58500).
Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu stated that Israeli forces are dismantling Hezbollah’s capabilities and warned of a strong response if Iran attacks again (Source: @amitsegal/61980).
Netanyahu declared that the fire on the Iranian front is currently restrained, but Israel will respond forcefully if Iran fires again (Source: @amitsegal/61982).
The IDF announced the elimination of three senior Palestinian Islamic Jihad militants during the weekend (Source: @idf_telegram/24238).
Civil defense alerts were issued in Eilat after a suspected UAV intrusion and a possible missile launch from Yemen, with sirens later lifted (Source: @N12chat/211771).
The Israeli Defense Forces reported detecting a ballistic missile launch from Iran, with sirens expected in northern Israel (Source: @manniefabian/58500).
Netanyahu warned that any attack on northern Israeli communities will be met with retaliation in the form of "Daachiyah" in Beirut, linking the north‑south front (Source: @amitsegal/61975).
Israeli officials reiterated that Iran’s attempts to connect Lebanon and Iran for attacks on Israel will be answered with great force, referencing a recent incident (Source: @amitsegal/61975).
No direct operational events in Gaza or the West Bank were detailed in the sourced messages.

Hezbollah launched three rockets toward Israeli troops in southern Lebanon; two were intercepted by Israeli air defenses, the third struck near forces without causing injuries (Source: @manniefabian/58511).

The Iranian resistance statement condemned Israeli violations of the cease‑fire in southern Lebanon, reporting casualties among civilians and describing Israeli artillery targeting the town of Yehmar al‑Shqif (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/367982).
Israel issued an urgent evacuation warning for residents of southern Lebanon’s Zaqouq al‑Mafadi area, citing violations of the cease‑fire by Hezbollah (Source: @VahidOnline).

Israeli Defense Minister Yoav Gallant reiterated that any Iranian‑linked aggression from Lebanon will be met with strong retaliation, referencing a recent Israeli strike in Beirut (Source: @amitsegal/61975).

A coordinated Iranian attack coincided with Hezbollah rocket fire from Lebanon, and reports indicated simultaneous operations in Iraq, including the downing of an American MQ‑9 drone near Karbala using shoulder‑launched missiles (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/368015).

Iraqi sources observed rapid mobilization of militia forces and the creation of “air ambushes” ahead of the Iranian missile launch, suggesting heightened readiness on the ground (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/368015).
No direct Syrian government facilities were mentioned, but Iranian strikes targeted Iranian‑linked sites within Syria’s western region according to broader reports (implicit, not cited).
U.S. allies plan to seek President Trump’s approval at the G7 for a UK‑French de‑mining mission in the Strait of Hormuz, ready to deploy after any Iran deal (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3634).
Trump reportedly urged Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu to refrain from further retaliatory strikes, citing ongoing negotiations for a regional agreement (Source: @mamlekate/103648).
American officials denied U.S. involvement in the recent Israeli strikes on Iran and stated that Trump did not order any assistance for Israel’s missile defense (Source: @asafroz/50002).

key_developments: The IDF killed three senior Palestinian Islamic Jihad militants during the weekend, demonstrating intensified counter‑terror operations in the Gaza sector (Source: @idf_telegram/24238).

Israel’s air force struck nine Iranian air‑defence installations and three factories at a petrochemical complex in Mahshahr, targeting Iran’s missile‑production capabilities (Source: @idf_telegram/24232).
Hezbollah launched three rockets toward Israeli forces in southern Lebanon; two were intercepted and one landed near troops without causing casualties (Source: @manniefabian/58511).
Iran announced a formal cessation of armed operations against Israel but warned that any further aggression, especially from Lebanon, would trigger far harsher measures (Source: @shin_persian/6748).
Ballistic missiles were detected launching from Iran toward Israel, prompting sirens in northern Israel and confirming ongoing missile exchange (Source: @manniefabian/58500).
Eilat civil defense issued alerts after a suspected UAV intrusion and a possible missile launch from Yemen, later confirming the event’s conclusion (Source: @N12chat/211771).

U.S. allies are preparing a G7‑backed de‑mining operation in the Strait of Hormuz, contingent on a forthcoming Iran agreement, indicating broader regional security planning (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3634).
sections.threat_assessment: Iran continues to leverage both direct missile launches and proxy coordination across Iraq, Syria, and Lebanon, while maintaining a public stance of operational cessation that may be tactical. Hezbollah’s rocket fire, though limited, shows readiness to test Israeli defenses and could scale if Iranian pressure intensifies. Israel’s proactive strikes on Iranian air‑defence and petrochemical sites demonstrate a willingness to expand the conflict beyond its borders, raising the risk of broader regional escalation.
sections.regional_response: U.S. and Gulf partners are positioning diplomatic and operational tools, such as the proposed Strait of Hormuz de‑mining mission, to manage maritime security amid heightened tensions. Trump’s diplomatic outreach to Netanyahu suggests an attempt to temper Israeli retaliation while negotiations with Iran progress. Lebanese authorities face internal pressure as Israeli evacuation warnings and Hezbollah attacks heighten civilian risk in the south.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Iranian missile trajectories and Iraqi militia air‑ambush activities is critical for early warning of coordinated attacks. Continued surveillance of Hezbollah launch sites in southern Lebanon is needed to assess escalation potential. Israeli civil‑defence alerts in Eilat indicate a pattern of low‑altitude UAV threats that may precede larger missile strikes.
